content.full_text:
December 24, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for informal assistance.

To whom it may concern,

The purpose of this letter is to request informal assistance pursuant to 2 Cal. Code of Regs § 18329(c) with respect to ascertaining sources of income to Oceanside City Council Member Colleen Richardson O'Harra.

To the best of my knowledge the facts relating to this request are as follows:

Council Member O'Harra was elected to the Oceanside City Council in November 1992. She is a member of the California Bar and practices in association with the Oceanside law firm of Gore, Grosse, Greenman & Lacy. She is not a partner in the firm. Her relationship with the firm is unique among the lawyers in that firm. Unlike other associates in the firm who receive a salary plus a potential bonus from firm profits, Council Member O'Harra is compensated based upon a percentage of the gross income received solely from her billings. Although she is guaranteed a certain base salary, the compensation based upon the percentage of the gross income from her billings has always been more than the base salary. Council Member O'Harra does not share in the profits of the firm as a whole. The firm pays the cost of professional insurance, secretarial service, office space, library use, and other office services. Council Member O'Harra is married and her spouse is employed. Her community property share of her spouse's income is not a basis for this inquiry.

Periodically, persons who are clients of Council Member O'Harra and of the firm are either directly or indirectly involved in governmental decisions of the City of Oceanside. Under the regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission the effect of a decision is material if a source of income is directly involved in a decision before the official's agency or there is a nexus between the purpose for which the official receives income and the governmental decision [2 Cal. Code of Regs. § 18702.1] or if a source of income is indirectly involved in a decision and the decision will have certain threshold affects [2 Cal. Code of Regs.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
Re: Request for informal assistance
December 24, 1992
Page 2

§ 18702.6]. We are requesting assistance in order to determine whether a person (including a business entity) who is a source of income to the law firm would be considered a source of income to Council Member O'Harra, and if so under what circumstances.

To the extent that you can provide prior advise letters relating to this matter and similar matters that would be most helpful. If there is a copying charge for the provision of those letters, please let me know so that payment for those copies can be made.

If you have any questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

Daniel S. Hentschke
Interim City Attorney

cc: Council Member O'Harra
